markers.evidence_type: Known marker cited in atlas annotation and literature
Known marker cited in atlas annotation and literature
MHC class II molecule expressed by LCs

other_findings.finding: LC precursors are first identified in human epidermis at 7-9 weeks estimated gestational age (EGA) based on CD45, HLA-DR, CD1c and ATPase expression. The defining LC markers CD207/langerin, CD1a and Birbeck granules are detectable only after 11 weeks EGA.

LCs are grouped with DCs in the atlas myeloid compartment. The atlas focuses primarily on macrophage populations for immune-morphogenesis analysis; LCs are identified as a distinct broad cell lineage in the UMAP but are not the focus of deep functional analysis.
other_findings.source_file: Gopee et al. (2024), atlas paper main text and figures

other_findings.finding: In human prenatal skin, LCs go through morphological and phenotypic changes: they grow larger, more dendritic, gain expression of Langerin and CD1a, and form Birbeck granules in a stepwise manner. LCs are uniformly dendritic from 8 PCW.

evidence_quotes.context: Abbreviation definition in UMAP visualization legend
